title: Fee Proposal — Sprint 0 Kickoff
granola_summary: Sprint 0 kickoff for the AL Fee Proposal Module 1. Discussed CMap data gap, Supabase schema, and Danny's engagement model.
transcript:
Oliver: Right, so Sprint 0 officially starts today. Felipe, Danny — good to have you both here. The main thing I want to nail down is what we're actually building in the next three weeks before the next commercial review with Jon.

Felipe: Agreed. From my side, the critical path is the CMap data gap. Without past project actuals in Supabase, the fee calculation won't have the benchmarks it needs. Danny, where are we on the CMap API?

Danny: I spoke to CMap on Friday. Their REST API does expose historical project financials — it's a paid tier, plan is called CMap Enterprise Connect. I've got a sandbox account. The endpoint structure is straightforward: projects, phases, cost codes, actuals by month. We can pull everything we need. The question is whether AL is on the right CMap plan.

Oliver: We're on the standard plan currently. I'll need to check with Ellen whether we can upgrade or if there's a budget conversation with Jon first.

Felipe: Let's flag that as a gated dependency. If CMap upgrade isn't approved, we build the ETL against a CSV export instead — slower but achieves the same thing for the prototype. Either way, Danny, can you have the Supabase schema drafted by Thursday?

Danny: Yes, I'll have a schema doc by Thursday EOD. I'll cover the projects table, phases, cost codes, and the fee benchmark view.

Oliver: Good. On the scope side — the thing I keep coming back to is the sixty-odd scope items that live in my head and nowhere else. That's the risk. If something happens to me, the firm can't generate a credible fee proposal. Felipe, what's the plan there?

Felipe: That's exactly what the scope-draft skill solves. We're going to run a structured session — probably next week — where you and I go through the scope items together and I extract them into a structured format that lives in Supabase. Then the skill can surface them automatically based on project type and RIBA stage. We should schedule that for Wednesday if you're free.

Oliver: Wednesday works. Let's say 10am.

Felipe: Noted. Final thing — Danny, I want to make sure we're aligned on your engagement model. You're coming in as a technical co-builder on this sprint. Are you comfortable with the current arrangement, or do we need to formalise something before we go further?

Danny: I think we need a brief email from Molior confirming the scope and rate before Sprint 0 ends. Nothing complex — just so there's a paper trail before we start building the production version.

Felipe: Agreed. I'll send that today.

Oliver: Perfect. So: Danny has the Supabase schema by Thursday, scope session with Felipe on Wednesday 10am, and Felipe sends Danny the engagement confirmation today. CMap upgrade is gated on Ellen and Jon. Next full team check-in — let's say Monday the 22nd?

Felipe: Monday the 22nd works for me.

Danny: Same.

Oliver: Great. Let's get moving.
